growth_records.medium: Minimal defined medium with glucose as sole carbon source (phosphate-buffered, pH 7)

up.method: HPLC (Aminex-87H); uptake rate = slope of [glucose] vs cell density

sec.method: HPLC; described as a minor product

mfa.note: Fig. 1 FBA flux map for C. pasteurianum; per-product relative fluxes only in figure image; absolute values in SI Appendix Table S6

growth_records.cite: Mueller, Elliott P. et al. Metabolic controls on the carbon isotope fractionations of bacterial fermentation. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2025). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2511040122. [PMC12541407]
growth_records.snippet: C. pasteurianum made acetate, butyrate, and a minor amount of formate. ... Growth rates for E. coli, V. fischeri, C. pasteurianum are similar at 0.47, 0.434, and 0.473 h-1.
growth_records.notes: mu=0.473 h-1 reported (OD600, SI Fig. S1). doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.473 = 1.465 h. Butyrate-type clostridial fermentation of glucose; products acetate + butyrate + minor formate measured by HPLC. Strain not named in main text. Phosphate buffer pH 7. Full medium in SI Tables S1-S5 -> not stated. Absolute rates in SI Table S6; rate fields null (not invented). Growth temperature not stated in main text.
